Motion For Default Judgment

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Asher Ahmed, Esq., Infinty Law Group, Telephone 415-426-3580, appearing for Plaintiff;
Robert Schoen, Esq., Legal Assistance to the Elderly, Inc., Telephone 415-538-333, appearing for Defendant Roger Cardenas;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROGER CARDENAS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/FOR LEAVE TO DEFEND

Defendant Roger Cardenas, dba Bay Cab and Arrow Checker Cab's motion to vacate default and to quash service of summons is granted. Although Plaintiff San Francisco Independent Taxi Association acted with "reasonable diligence" in attempting to serve Defendant, Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the individual who received the summons and complaint was the person apparently in charge of Defendant's "usual place of business." (Code Civ. Proc. 415.20(b).) Defendant shows that he has only a tenuous relationship with National Cab Company at 2270 McKinnon Avenue in that he lodges his taxi medallion with them, but does not maintain a desk or office space at that address. (Cardenas Decl. 4.) Moreover, Plaintiff has not shown that the person who received substitute service is someone whose "relationship with the person to be served makes it more likely than not that they will deliver process to the named party." (Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387, 1393.) Plaintiff has only shown that the summons and complaint were left with an unnamed dispatcher. Given Defendant's representations that he does not know "John Doe," that he did not receive actual notice of service, and that his illness prevented him from personally accepting service at an earlier time, the Court concludes that substitute service was not properly effectuated. Therefore, service of summons is quashed. (Code Civ. Proc. 418.10.)

As a result, the Court was without jurisdiction when it entered default against Defendant on August 9, 2018, and that default is vacated as void pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d). (See Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1249.) Plaintiff San Francisco Independent Taxi Association's motion for default judgment shall be taken off calendar.

Defendant's sworn declaration indicates that he had serious health issues until August 2018. Defendant is therefore ordered to accept service of Plaintiff's complaint at his home address of 34 Liberty Street, #2 or through other means agreed upon by the parties.

Robert Schoen, counsel for Defendant Roger Cardenas agrees to accept service on defendant's behalf.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)
